location.office_time_required: unclear; in-person onboarding required if hired

level_signals: 5+ years in performance engineering, performance TPM, platform TPM, or SRE
Horizontal, high-visibility platform-level role spanning multiple product surfaces
Base salary range $185K–$317K USD consistent with senior IC at a major tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in performance engineering, performance TPM, platform TPM, or SRE with hands-on experience shipping performance programs for SaaS products
Demonstrated experience with load, stress, performance or scalability testing and new-build comparisons
Deep familiarity with web performance (FCP, LCP), rendering/FPS, WASM memory, mobile profiling (Xcode Instruments, Android Profiler)
CI/CD and test automation experience: integrating performance tests into pipelines
Distributed systems, cloud infra (AWS), autoscaling, and capacity planning
Hands-on prior work with LLMs, prompt engineering, or agent platforms

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with MLops or LLMs: inference latency tradeoffs, batching, caching, etc
Background as a performance engineer (not just TPM) with code or test harness contributions.
Experience with large enterprise Planning / customer load tests.
Familiarity with real-time collaborative systems and GPU/WASM performance constraints.

scope.surface: Platform-level performance programs spanning Figma's flagship products and AI features; desktop, browser, native mobile (React Native / WebView), and WASM contexts
primary_metrics_inferred: Model inference latency, throughput, cost, and reliability (AI features)
SEV mitigation and safe AI rollout
core_responsibilities: Own performance prevention, diagnostics, and safe rollout for flagship products and next-gen AI features
Manage horizontal performance programs spanning desktop, browser, and mobile
Drive observability/telemetry programs and regression prevention (performance CI)
Run cross-functional performance forums and SEV mitigation
Lead safe rollout of AI capabilities
Support CE Planning (customer engineering / enterprise readiness)

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area (performance programs across flagship + AI)
Encourages candidates whose experience doesn't perfectly align to still apply
Comprehensive benefits package (health, equity, PTO, L&D stipend, WFH stipend, parental leave, etc.)
red_flags: Extremely wide base salary range ($185K–$317K) offers limited signal on actual compensation level for a given candidate
Remote pay is localized at 80–100% of range, which may significantly reduce compensation for candidates outside SF/NY
In-person onboarding required even for fully remote hires — travel commitment not quantified

JD says 'Hands-on prior work with LLMs, prompt engineering, or agent platforms' is a must-have, but the overall tone and context could make this ambiguous as to whether it is truly required or preferred

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years + comp + program scope)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated as metrics)
ambiguities: The JD states the role 'can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States' — treated as hybrid for hub-based employees and remote-US eligible overall; location.type set to 'hybrid' to reflect hub option, with remote_us_eligible: true.

LLMs / prompt engineering / agent platforms appears in the 'We'd love to hear from you if you have' section alongside other clearly hard requirements, making its severity ambiguous. Classified as hard (must_have) per instructions, and flagged here.
The pay range ($185K–$317K) is unusually wide; it may encompass multiple levels or be pre-localization adjustment.
